overview.brief_summary: A sophisticated attack targeting transportation systems, utilizing multiple techniques to gain initial access, maintain persistence, and disrupt operations.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Process Scheduled Task Command & Control Server Application Log Target System Artifact User Account Network Traffic File DNS Record
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method Artificial Intelligence Reg.exe Utility Process Creation (Mechanism) Web Shell Application Log Content (Mechanism) User Evasion User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Mimikatz Tool File Access (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about transportation systems using artificial intelligence to analyze open-source intelligence.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker identifies vulnerabilities in transportation system networks by monitoring process activity using reg.exe utility.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attacker creates a custom backdoor for initial access by developing malicious software deployment tools.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker infects SolarWinds software updates to gain initial access to transportation system networks.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker uses a compromised website to infect transportation system employees' devices.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker injects malware into transportation system's IT environment.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to maintain persistence in the transportation system.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: The attacker establishes a persistent web shell on transportation system servers.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker gains elevated privileges using public-facing application vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attacker avoids detection by anti-virus software.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker guesses credentials of transportation system employees.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC-226: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: The attacker dumps credentials from the OS.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally using valid accounts.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data from local systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates data over alternative protocols.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts transportation system operations.

Scenario.summary: The SolarWinds Compromise scenario involves a complex attack chain targeting transportation systems, utilizing multiple techniques to gain initial access, maintain persistence, and disrupt operations.
